title: neottia grandiflora
intro: Neottia grandiflora, also known as the large-flowered toothwort, is a perennial orchid native to China and other parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing pain and swelling, and supporting immune function. In traditional Chinese medicine, Neottia grandiflora is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
